description: All framing, sheathing, and structural blocking are complete, inspected, and approved before MEP rough-in crews begin work.

categories.name: Framing Inspection & Approvals
items: Baldwin County framing inspection scheduled, passed, and inspection card signed and posted on-site
Superintendent walk performed before inspector arrival — all open items resolved
Structural engineer's site visit completed and written sign-off on file (beam pockets, header spans, LVL placement, hold-downs)
All required engineered lumber (LVL, PSL, I-joists) installed per stamped structural drawings — deviations documented and EOR-approved
Shear wall locations confirmed as-built against structural drawings, all required Simpson Strong-Wall or panel segments nailed per schedule

categories.name: Structural & Connection Quality
items: All headers, double top plates, and king/jack stud configurations verified per plan at every opening
Roof-to-wall connections confirmed: hurricane straps or clips (H2.5A or per wind-load schedule) installed at every rafter/truss bearing point per Baldwin County wind zone requirements
Floor system: rim joist blocking, joist hangers fully nailed (no missing or partially driven fasteners), blocking at bearing points per schedule
All hold-down hardware (HDU/PHD anchors) installed, snugged, and rod couplers accessible for MEP routing coordination
Stair framing: rise/run uniform within 3/8-inch total variation, stringers bearing minimum 3-1/2 inches on framing, blocking complete
Cantilevered elements and balcony framing confirmed as-built per engineer's detail — no unapproved field changes

categories.name: Sheathing & Weather Barrier
items: All wall sheathing (typically 7/16" or 15/32" OSB/plywood) installed with correct nail pattern per shear schedule — 6" field, 3" panel edges where required
Roof sheathing fully applied, H-clips in place at unsupported edges, no gaps exceeding 1/8" at panel joints
House wrap (Tyvek or equal) fully installed, lapped correctly (horizontal seams lapped over lower course), taped at all seams and penetrations
Flashing installed at all window and door rough openings (sill pan flashing, head flashing) prior to window installation
All penetrations through sheathing (plumbing, HVAC, electrical stub-outs) temporarily sealed or flagged for sub-specific flashing during rough-in

categories.name: Rough Opening Verification
items: All window rough openings measured and confirmed against approved window schedule — width and height within +0" / -1/4" of scheduled R.O.
All exterior door rough openings confirmed against door schedule; sill heights at exterior doors verified for threshold and finish-floor elevation
Interior door rough openings sized per door schedule — width and height allowing for specified jamb and finish tolerances
Garage door rough openings (width, height, header bearing) confirmed against door manufacturer's installation requirements
All R.O.s confirmed plumb and square (diagonal measurement differential ≤ 1/8" per 4-foot span)

categories.name: Blocking & Backing
items: Backing installed for all wall-mounted TV locations per client's AV layout — minimum 3/4" plywood, 48" × 32" or per AV contractor's spec
Blocking installed for all grab bar locations (master bath, secondary baths) per client's accessibility requests and ADA-reference dimensions even if not code-required
Cabinet backing (3/4" ply) installed at all upper cabinet walls in kitchen, butler's pantry, laundry, and mudroom per cabinet layout
Blocking installed for all ceiling fans, pendant locations, and heavy chandeliers exceeding 35 lbs — backer rated for fixture weight
Stair handrail and guardrail blocking installed per rail sub's requirements
Blocking for all wall-mounted plumbing fixtures (floating vanities, wall-hung toilets, body-spray valves) confirmed with plumber prior to inspection sign-off

categories.name: MEP Pre-Rough-In Coordination
items: Superintendent walk with lead plumber, HVAC contractor, and electrician completed — chase locations, beam conflicts, and routing paths agreed before any cutting begins
Critical floor-joist boring and notching rules communicated to all MEP subs: notches in top/bottom 1/3 of joist prohibited, max bore 1/3 joist depth, per IRC R802/R502 and structural drawings
HVAC duct chase and air handler closet framed per mechanical layout — clearances confirmed sufficient for equipment access and filter pull
Attic access framed and sized per HVAC contractor requirements for equipment installation and future service (minimum 22" × 30" unless larger equipment requires it)
Electrical panel location framing confirmed: minimum 36" clear working space in front, dedicated wall surface clear of plumbing penetrations
All subs have current as-built framing photos uploaded to project Drive folder before first crew returns

categories.name: Plumb, Level & Tolerance Check
items: Exterior walls checked for plumb: tolerance ≤ 1/4" in 8 feet on all walls to be tiled or receive large-format stone
All bearing walls and columns checked for plumb using 6-foot level or laser — out-of-plumb conditions > 1/4" in 8' documented and corrected before MEP rough-in
Floor system checked for level across entire footprint: tolerance ≤ 3/16" in 10 feet, ≤ 1/8" in 6 feet in kitchen and master bath where large-format tile is specified
Ceiling joist/truss bottom chord level verified in great room, kitchen, and master suite — high/low points noted and sistered or shimmed as required
Superintendent final signature on framing punch list confirming all tolerance items resolved or formally accepted
